task_completion.description: Import the character and nametag assets into the OpenToonz scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Xsheet or Timeline shows columns/layers for both 'dwanko_run' and 'nametag'. The assets should be visible in the main viewer.

task_completion.description: Create a hierarchical parenting connection between the nametag and the character.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stage Schematic window is open and shows a connecting line (link) from the 'nametag' node (child) to the 'dwanko_run' node (parent), indicating a rigging relationship.

task_completion.description: Animate the character moving from the left side of the screen to the right over 20-40 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Xsheet or Timeline shows keyframe icons for the 'dwanko_run' column starting at frame 1 and ending between frames 20 and 40. The viewer should show the character's position shifted to the right in later frames.

task_completion.description: Ensure the nametag follows the character via the parenting connection rather than manual keyframing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'nametag' column in the Xsheet/Timeline lacks multiple motion keyframes across the 20-40 frame span, relying instead on the parent's movement. The nametag should appear attached to the character in the viewer during the animation.

task_completion.description: Render the animation as an MP4 to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog shows the save path as '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/' with the name 'rigged_run' and format 'mp4', or a terminal/file manager window shows the 'rigged_run.mp4' file successfully created in that directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to set up the scene, rigging, and animation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz interface (e.g., Schematic view, Xsheet, Viewer, Output Settings) rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent rendered the video using OpenToonz rather than copying a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar, the OpenToonz Output Settings window, or the Render command being executed within the OpenToonz UI is visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not cheat the parenting requirement by manually animating the nametag frame-by-frame.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Stage Schematic clearly shows the parenting link, and the Xsheet does not show a dense sequence of manual keyframes on the nametag layer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The nametag asset is located at /home/ga/Desktop/nametag.png. The character asset is a PNG image located at /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.png. The final rendered video must be saved exactly to /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/rigged_run.mp4.
